title: Recruitment Supervisor Job at Lumos Nigeria
content:
Lumos Nigeria is a leading provider of high-quality solar home systems, offering a range of reliable and affordable energy solutions designed to power households and small businesses across Nigeria. The company focuses on delivering clean, renewable electricity through innovative solar technology, helping customers reduce dependence on unreliable grid power and fuel generators. With a strong emphasis on accessibility and convenience, Lumos Nigeria combines durable equipment with flexible payment options, making sustainable energy more attainable for everyday users while supporting economic productivity and improved quality of life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Recruitment Supervisor
     

            
    



Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Recruitment Supervisor will be responsible for driving and managing end-to-end recruitment activities across the organization.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong background in high-volume recruitment, particularly for sales and field-based roles, and possess extensive networks across key regions in Nigeria.
	The successful candidate will play a critical role in attracting, assessing, and hiring top talent while ensuring recruitment targets are met within agreed timelines.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the full recruitment lifecycle from manpower requisition to onboarding.
	Develop and implement effective sourcing strategies to attract qualified candidates.
	Create and publish job advertisements across various recruitment channels.
	Screen and evaluate CVs to identify suitable candidates for open positions.
	Conduct competency-based interviews and coordinate hiring assessments.
	Manage high-volume recruitment campaigns for sales and field operations roles.
	Build and maintain a strong talent pipeline for current and future hiring needs.
	Coordinate recruitment activities across the South-West, South-East, and South-South regions.
	Partner with hiring managers to understand workforce requirements and hiring priorities.
	Track recruitment metrics and prepare periodic recruitment reports.
	Ensure a positive candidate experience throughout the recruitment process.
	Support employer branding initiatives and recruitment marketing activities.
	Maintain accurate recruitment records and ensure compliance with company policies.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of proven recruitment experience.
	Demonstrated experience recruiting for Sales Executives, Merchandisers, Marketers, Territory Managers, and other field sales positions.
	Strong recruitment network across the South-West, South-East, and South-South regions of Nigeria.
	Proven ability to manage large-scale and high-volume recruitment projects.
	Previous experience working in a leading outsourcing or recruitment firm is highly desirable and will be considered an added advantage
	Excellent candidate sourcing skills using job boards, social media, referrals, and professional networks.
	Strong interviewing, assessment, and candidate evaluation capabilities.
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and relationship-building skills.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and meet aggressive hiring timelines.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) is an advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Talent Acquisition
	Candidate Sourcing
	Interviewing and Assessment
	Relationship Building
	Recruitment Analytics
	Time Management
	Communication Skills
	Attention to Detail.


Compensation


	Competitive net salary of N350,000 – N400,000 per month (negotiable).
	Opportunity to work with a fast-growing and innovative organization.
	Professional growth and career development opportunities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position  and current Location as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Bosak Microfinance Bank
content:
Bosak Microfinance Bank is dedicated to promoting financial inclusion among women and low-income entrepreneurs through tailored micro?credit, savings, and insurance products. With subsidairies in Ekiti and Kogi, known as Bosak Grassroot Empowerment Initiative, recognized for its impact in Nigeria’s microfinance sector.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Ado-Ekiti, Ekiti
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
     

            
    




	Provide adequate assistance in ensuring reference/guarantor letters of new hires are sent one week following submission of documentation.
	Ensure files/ staff documents are properly filed.
	Update new staff and All staff record on Excel.
	Update new and exited staff record.
	Ensure that all staff documentations and details are up to date and easily retrievable.
	Processing staff members leave form.
	Processing signing of offer letters of new hires.
	Filling and compilation of staff members Annual Appraisal form.
	Follow up on internal correspondent with other departments.
	Managing and compiling daily staff attendance record.
	Preparation of statutory remittances such as pension, NSITF & ITF schedule and should be sent for approval not later than 30th of the month.
	Other tasks as assigned.


Benefits
We offer the following Benefits:


	Salary: N100,000 – N140,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave & Passage Allowance


Why Work with Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited?


	If you desire to work in an organization where employee motivation is key and passion is rewarded, then Bosak MFB might be the place for you.
	We are committed to building an open and rewarding work environment where all employees have equal opportunities for growth to realize their full potential.
	Our remuneration and employee welfare packages are among the best in the industry.


Application Closing Date: 31st July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Human Resource Officer Ekiti” as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Internal Control Officer

Job Location: Ekiti 

Responsibilties


	Conducting confidential investigations on control breaches.
	Report Internal Control deficiencies identified on the field work with marketers and Loan Officers.
	Ensure adequate care and maintenance of bank’s properties.
	Monitors organization wide compliance with laid down policies, processes and procedure to ensure adherence.
	Review & manage the day-to-day activities attendance register and operational issues.
	Ensure that strategies defined are implemented by Internal Audit Team for accomplishment of the department’s objectives.
	Carry out specific management assigned tasks.
	Coordinate Internal Control Officers in the different regions and ensuring that their activities are carried out as outlined.
	Assist in carrying out Full Scale Inspection & Audit.
	Calling over of transactions.
	Quality control.
	Checking of Loan Officer’s daily transactions.
	Report all form of infraction affecting the business arising from daily situations on a weekly basis.
	Spot check on Operations & Outreach
	Assess the primary risks facing the bank and identify risk mitigation measures.
	Managing the risks associated with the different Units in the bank (Enterprise Risk Management) and following up to ensure compliance on all observations.
	Handles customer’s complaints via Toll-Free Telephone Line, investigates arising situations and reports to the Unit Head for the bank’s management action.


Why Work with Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited?


	If you desire to work in an organization where employee motivation is key and passion is rewarded, then Bosak MFB might be the place for you.
	We are committed to building an open and rewarding work environment where all employees have equal opportunities for growth to realize their full potential.
	Our remuneration and employee welfare packages are among the best in the industry.


Benefits
We offer the following Benefits:


	Salary: N150,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave & Passage Allowance
	13th Month.


Application Closing Date: 29th July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Internel Control Officer Ekiti” as the subject of the email.

 

3.) Credit Risk Officer

Job Location: Ikole, Ekiti
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Preparations of loans to be disburse on Daily basis.
	Loan Appraisal / Loan Analysis.
	Pre - chat with customers.
	Preparation of confirmation list/credit search list of approved loan forms.
	Recording of Loans disbursed manually
	Recording of all customers disbursed to in Membership list.
	Generation of active running loans report for BAFL
	Generation of BTL and BATSL Expected report for Delinquency Management Officer.
	Generation of report for loans booked as at the end of every week (Fridays).
	Field disbursement
	Booking and disbursing of Loans.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualification with 2 - 5 years of experience.


Benefits


	Salary: N150,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave & Passage Allowance
	13th Month.


Application Closing Date: 31st August, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Credit Risk Officer, Ekiti” as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

School Secretary Job at Arrupe Jesuit College




        
    

        
        Arrupe Jesuit College is a non-profit co-educational boarding Catholic and Jesuit secondary school that welcomes young men and women of diverse cultural and religious backgrounds.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: School Secretary
Job Location: Abbi, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	To perform a variety of secretarial, clerical and administrative duties in support of the operations of the school.
	Set up and maintain filing systems for students- paper and electronic.
	Create, maintain and enter information into database.
	Locate and attach appropriate files to incoming correspondence requiring a response.
	Locate and provide data as requested.
	Schedule and confirm appointments for staff members with the Principal and President.
	Maintain appointment and event calendars.
	Write minutes of meetings.
	Over and above all, every staff in AJC is a role model to the students.
	Accommodation is available.


Requirements /Qualifications


	B.A in English
	Any higher Degree or qualification, especially in English is an advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Applications, CV, and scanned copies of their credentials to: [email protected] and copied to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Acquisition Specialist",
  "description": "At Careers Verified, we go beyond narrowly defined client&rsquo;s issues, we create opportunities for our clients, candidates, colleagues, collaborators, and the community. Through a network of diversified but highly complementary line of business, we help organizations find critical talent, access cutting-edge advice and get the innovative support they require to grow in a challenging market. We partner with businesses and organizations of varying size and structure across diverse sectors, such as Oil and Gas, Engineering, Finance, Telecom, FMCG, Legal, Technology, Healthcare, Travel and Aviation etc. to enhance business performance and to optimize for growth to achieve exceptional results. What sets us apart is the depth of our capabilities, creative problem solving approach and proven track record in our practice area combined with in-depth knowledge of the realities of the industries we serve.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Acquisition Specialist&nbsp;
Job Location: Nationwide
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Customer Acquisition Specialist to drive customer acquisition, promote financial products and services, and build long-term relationships with clients.
	The ideal candidate will identify sales opportunities, educate customers on financial solutions, meet sales targets, and ensure excellent customer service while maintaining compliance with regulatory and company policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and acquire new customers for the organization&#39;s financial products and services.
	Promote products such as savings accounts, loans, investment products, insurance, digital banking solutions, and other financial services.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with new and existing customers.
	Conduct customer needs assessments and recommend appropriate financial solutions.
	Generate leads through field marketing, referrals, networking, and other business development activities.
	Meet and exceed assigned monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets.
	Prepare and deliver presentations on financial products to individuals, SMEs, and corporate clients.
	Follow up on prospects and convert leads into active customers.
	Ensure accurate completion and submission of customer documentation.
	Maintain a healthy sales pipeline and update customer information in the CRM system.
	Address customer inquiries, complaints, and service requests professionally.
	Stay informed about market trends, competitor activities, and new financial products.
	Ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices.
	Prepare regular sales and activity reports for management.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Monthly sales revenue achieved.
	Number of new customers acquired.
	Lead conversion rate.
	Loan or financial product disbursement volume.
	Customer retention rate.
	Cross-selling and upselling performance.
	Customer satisfaction scores.
	Quality and accuracy of documentation.
	Compliance with company policies and regulatory standards.
	Timeliness of reporting and sales activities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	1 year of experience in sales, business development, customer relationship management, or financial services.
	Experience in banking, fintech, microfinance, insurance, or consumer lending is an added advantage.
	Professional certifications in sales or finance are an added advantage.


Required Skills:


	Strong sales and negotiation skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Customer-focused with strong relationship management abilities.
	Ability to identify customer needs and recommend suitable financial solutions.
	Strong presentation and persuasion skills.
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM applications.
	Good time management and organizational skills.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
",

Customer Acquisition Specialist Job at Careers Verified




        
    

        
        
     

            
    

At Careers Verified, we go beyond narrowly defined client’s issues, we create opportunities for our clients, candidates, colleagues, collaborators, and the community. Through a network of diversified but highly complementary line of business, we help organizations find critical talent, access cutting-edge advice and get the innovative support they require to grow in a challenging market. We partner with businesses and organizations of varying size and structure across diverse sectors, such as Oil and Gas, Engineering, Finance, Telecom, FMCG, Legal, Technology, Healthcare, Travel and Aviation etc. to enhance business performance and to optimize for growth to achieve exceptional results. What sets us apart is the depth of our capabilities, creative problem solving approach and proven track record in our practice area combined with in-depth knowledge of the realities of the industries we serve.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Acquisition Specialist 
Job Location: Nationwide
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Customer Acquisition Specialist to drive customer acquisition, promote financial products and services, and build long-term relationships with clients.
	The ideal candidate will identify sales opportunities, educate customers on financial solutions, meet sales targets, and ensure excellent customer service while maintaining compliance with regulatory and company policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and acquire new customers for the organization's financial products and services.
	Promote products such as savings accounts, loans, investment products, insurance, digital banking solutions, and other financial services.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with new and existing customers.
	Conduct customer needs assessments and recommend appropriate financial solutions.
	Generate leads through field marketing, referrals, networking, and other business development activities.
	Meet and exceed assigned monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets.
	Prepare and deliver presentations on financial products to individuals, SMEs, and corporate clients.
	Follow up on prospects and convert leads into active customers.
	Ensure accurate completion and submission of customer documentation.
	Maintain a healthy sales pipeline and update customer information in the CRM system.
	Address customer inquiries, complaints, and service requests professionally.
	Stay informed about market trends, competitor activities, and new financial products.
	Ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices.
	Prepare regular sales and activity reports for management.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Monthly sales revenue achieved.
	Number of new customers acquired.
	Lead conversion rate.
	Loan or financial product disbursement volume.
	Customer retention rate.
	Cross-selling and upselling performance.
	Customer satisfaction scores.
	Quality and accuracy of documentation.
	Compliance with company policies and regulatory standards.
	Timeliness of reporting and sales activities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	1 year of experience in sales, business development, customer relationship management, or financial services.
	Experience in banking, fintech, microfinance, insurance, or consumer lending is an added advantage.
	Professional certifications in sales or finance are an added advantage.


Required Skills:


	Strong sales and negotiation skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Customer-focused with strong relationship management abilities.
	Ability to identify customer needs and recommend suitable financial solutions.
	Strong presentation and persuasion skills.
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM applications.
